compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why
`test_silent_success.py::test_reask_prompt_includes_bounded_prior_output` pins the private
constant `_FAULTY_OUTPUT_SNIPPET_CHARS == 2000` and greps `inspect.getsource(_pai_structured)`
for prompt fragments (`:92-99`); it never runs a retry, so it passes even if the reask is
broken. VALIDATED. The suite already uses `FunctionModel` seams, so a behavioral test is
straightforward.

## What
Drive `_pai_structured` with a model that returns malformed output on turn 1, capture the
turn-2 request, and assert the reask embeds the prior reply, bounded, and marked truncated
only when necessary.

## Scope
- `tests/unit/test_silent_success.py`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test forces a malformed first reply and captures the actual second model request.
- [ ] Asserts prior output is present, bounded to the cap, and `[truncated]` appears only
      when the prior output exceeded the cap.
- [ ] No assertion on the private constant name or on `_pai_structured` source text.
- [ ] `make test` green.

file_impact.reason: replace constant/getsource substring test with behavioral retry-capture

compiled_state.title: Replace bounded-reask test with a behavioral capture of the retry request
